payload.answer: The new memory is a duplicate of the existing memory. It restates the same fact with minor wording/punctuation variation and should not create a new durable memory card.
citations: Existing: "Sam from Goldman mentioned that he likes Bill Evans"
New: "Sam from Goldman said he likes Bill Evans"

entity_resolution.reason: The new statement appears to refer to the same person and same preference as the existing memory; no new entity resolution is needed.

details: Difference is only phrasing ("mentioned" vs "said") with no semantic change.

repair_options: Do not store a new memory card.
Optionally normalize wording to a canonical form if the system keeps a single representation.
